classes.name: Cashew Anthracnose
classes.description: A fungal disease causing dark, sunken lesions on leaves, twigs, and fruits. Spreads rapidly in humid conditions. Remove infected parts and apply copper-based fungicide.

classes.name: Cashew Gumosis
classes.description: Characterised by gum exudation from bark and branches, often caused by fungal or bacterial infection. Prune affected branches and apply wound sealant.

classes.name: Cashew Healthy
classes.description: No visible signs of disease or pest damage. The cashew plant appears to be in good health. Continue regular monitoring.

classes.name: Cashew Leaf Miner
classes.description: Larvae tunnel between leaf surfaces creating serpentine mines, reducing photosynthesis. Use neem-based sprays or introduce parasitic wasps as biological control.

classes.name: Cashew Red Rust
classes.description: Caused by algal infection (Cephaleuros), forming reddish-brown patches on leaves and stems. Improve air circulation and apply copper oxychloride.

classes.name: Cassava Bacterial Blight
classes.description: Angular water-soaked leaf spots that turn brown, with wilting and stem dieback. Use disease-free planting material and resistant varieties. Remove infected plants.

classes.name: Cassava Brown Spot
classes.description: Circular brown lesions with yellow halos on older leaves. Usually appears in mature plants. Maintain good field hygiene and use resistant cultivars.

classes.name: Cassava Green Mite
classes.description: Tiny green mites feed on leaf undersides causing chlorosis, leaf distortion, and defoliation. Introduce predatory mites or apply miticides during dry season.

classes.name: Cassava Healthy
classes.description: No visible signs of disease or pest damage. The cassava plant appears to be in good health. Continue regular monitoring.

classes.name: Cassava Mosaic
classes.description: Viral disease transmitted by whiteflies causing yellow-green mosaic patterns, leaf curling, and stunted growth. Use virus-free cuttings and resistant varieties. Control whitefly populations.

classes.name: Maize Fall Armyworm
classes.description: Larvae feed on leaves creating irregular window-pane damage and bore into the whorl. Apply Bt-based biopesticides early. Scout fields regularly during vegetative stage.

classes.name: Maize Grasshopper
classes.description: Grasshoppers chew leaf margins and can defoliate plants during outbreaks. Use bait traps, neem sprays, or targeted insecticides. Maintain field borders to reduce harborage.

classes.name: Maize Healthy
classes.description: No visible signs of disease or pest damage. The maize plant appears to be in good health. Continue regular monitoring.

classes.name: Maize Leaf Beetle
classes.description: Adults scrape leaf tissue creating elongated feeding scars parallel to veins. Heavy infestations reduce photosynthesis. Apply contact insecticides if threshold is exceeded.

classes.name: Maize Leaf Blight
classes.description: Long, elliptical grey-green lesions on leaves that expand and turn tan. Caused by Exserohilum turcicum. Use resistant hybrids and apply foliar fungicides at early infection.

classes.name: Maize Leaf Spot
classes.description: Small, round to oval tan spots with dark borders on leaves. Reduce humidity through proper spacing. Apply fungicides if infection is severe during grain fill.

classes.name: Maize Streak Virus
classes.description: Transmitted by leafhoppers, causing narrow yellow streaks along leaf veins. Stunts plant growth and reduces yield. Plant resistant varieties and control leafhopper vectors.

classes.name: Tomato Healthy
classes.description: No visible signs of disease or pest damage. The tomato plant appears to be in good health. Continue regular monitoring.

classes.name: Tomato Leaf Blight
classes.description: Dark brown to black water-soaked lesions that spread rapidly in wet conditions. Can destroy foliage within days. Apply preventive fungicides and avoid overhead irrigation.

classes.name: Tomato Leaf Curl
classes.description: Viral disease spread by whiteflies causing upward curling and thickening of leaves, stunted growth, and reduced fruit set. Control whiteflies and remove infected plants early.

classes.name: Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot
classes.description: Numerous small circular spots with dark margins and grey centres on lower leaves. Progresses upward. Remove affected leaves, improve air flow, and apply fungicides.

classes.name: Tomato Verticillium Wilt
classes.description: Soil-borne fungus causing yellowing and wilting of lower leaves, often one-sided. Brown discoloration in stem vascular tissue. Use resistant varieties and practice crop rotation.
